title: Pouteria lateriflora
intro: Pouteria lateriflora, also known as the White Cedar or Black Ironwood, is a medicinal plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, thanks to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, contributing to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Pouteria lateriflora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pouteria lateriflora is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Pouteria lateriflora is classified within the family Sapotaceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as sapote. This classification places Pouteria lateriflora among the tropical plants native to Central and South America. The Sapotaceae family is part of the order Ericales, which includes a wide variety of flowering plants. Pouteria lateriflora is further classified as a species within the genus Pouteria, a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in tropical regions. The classification of Pouteria lateriflora reflects its close evolutionary relationships with other species within the Sapotaceae family.
